utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hi ms. hernandez , dr. fisher , how are you ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hi dr. fisher . i'm doing okay except for my elbow here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so it's your right elbow ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it's my right elbow , yes .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . hey dragon , ms. hernandez is a 48-year-old female here for a right elbow . so , tell me what happened .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: well , i was , um , moving to a new home-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and i was , um , moving boxes from the truck into the house and i lifted a box up and then i felt like this popping-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hmm .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: and this strain as i was lifting it up onto the shelf .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and when- when did this happen ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: this was just yesterday .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and have you tried anything for it ? i mean ...

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: i put ice on it . and i've been taking ibuprofen , but it still hurts at lot .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , what makes it better or worse ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: the ice , when i have it on , is better .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: but , um , as soon as , you know , i take it off then it starts throbbing and hurting again .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . uh , let's review your past medical history . uh ... looks like you've got a history of anaphylaxis , is that correct ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes . yes , i do . yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: do you take any medications for it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: um , ep- ... just an epipen .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just epipen for anaphylaxis when you need it . um , and what surgeries have you had before ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah , so carotid . yeah-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . yeah , no , uh , your , uh , neck surgery . all right . well let's , uh , examine you here for a second . so it's your , uh , this elbow right here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and is it hurt- ... tender right around that area ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yes , it is .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . can you flex it or can you bend it ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: it hurts when i do that , yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . and go ahead and straighten out as much as you can .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: that's about it .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so there's some swelling there . and how about , uh , can you move your fingers okay ? does that hurt ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , that's fine .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: how about right over here ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , no that's fine . yeah .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . so on exam you've got some tenderness over your lateral epicondyle . uh , you have some swelling there and some redness . uh , you have some pain with flexion , extension of your elbow as well . uh , and you have some pain on the dorsal aspect of your- of your forearm as well . okay ? so let's look at your x-rays . hey dragon , show me the x-rays . all right . your x-ray of your elbow- it looks like , i mean , the bones are lined up properly . there's no fracture-

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: . it , uh , there's a little bit of swelling there on the lateral elbow but i do n't see any fracture , so that's good . so , looking at the x-ray and looking at your exam , uh , my diagnosis here would be lateral epicondylitis , and this is basically inflammation of this area where this tendon in- inserts . and probably that happened when you were moving those boxes . so we'll try some motrin , uh , about 800 milligrams every six hours . uh , i'll give you a sling for comfort , just so you can use it if- if it's causing a lot of pain .

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: hmm .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and it should get better , uh , in about , you know , in a couple of days it should be improved . and if it does n't get better , give us a call and we'll see you some time next week . okay ? so we'll give you a sling , we'll give you the motrin , i'll give you about , uh , 30 , uh , uh , 30 , uh , uh , medications for that . uh , do you have any questions ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no . thank you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , order the medications and the procedures . all right , why do n't you come with me and we'll get you signed out ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: okay , sounds good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey dragon , finalize the report .

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 48-year-old female presenting with right elbow pain.
Elbow pain
The patient felt a pop in her elbow while lifting boxes yesterday.
The patient has attempted to treat the pain with ice and ibuprofen without success.
The doctor asks what factors improve or worsen the patient's pain.
The patient has a history of anaphylaxis.
The doctor asks if the patient has a history of anaphylaxis.
The doctor asks if the patient takes any medications for anaphylaxis.
The patient uses an EpiPen for anaphylaxis.
The doctor asks about the patient's surgical history.
The patient has a history of neck surgery.
The doctor asks if the patient has tenderness in the elbow area.
The doctor asks if the patient can flex or bend the elbow.
The doctor asks if the patient can move her fingers without pain.
Physical exam shows tenderness over the lateral epicondyle, swelling, redness, pain with flexion and extension, and pain over the dorsal forearm.
X-rays show no fracture and mild effusion about the lateral aspect.
The doctor diagnoses the patient with lateral epicondylitis.
The doctor prescribes Motrin 800 mg to be taken every 6 hours.
The doctor recommends a sling for comfort.
The patient should follow up next week if the pain does not improve.
The doctor asks if the patient has any questions.

case_description: right elbow pain; anaphylaxis;hx neck surgery
